chapters.goal: Move Ren from isolation into reluctant guardianship.
chapters.summary: Ren discovers Mio in the ruins and chooses to protect her from ash spirits crossing the city.
chapters.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

key_objects.description: A rain-spotted paper lantern that keeps Mio visible and holds the last warm light in the city.
key_objects.name: Mio's Lantern
key_objects.significance: The lantern is the reason ash spirits pursue Mio and the reason Karakuri Vale can still remember hope.
key_objects.visual_notes: Handmade paper sides, bent bamboo frame, small flame that does not flicker in rain.

locations.description: A rain-slick ruined city of collapsed arcades, shrine gates, hanging wires, and dormant war machines.
locations.name: Karakuri Vale
rules: Ash spirits cannot cross a lit threshold.
locations.visual_notes: Broken rooflines, deep black alleys, white mist, lantern reflections in puddles.

chapter_outline.summary: Ren finds Mio and chooses to cross the city rather than abandon her.
chapter_outline.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

continuity_rules: Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
story_bible.target_audience: Teen and young adult manga readers
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.
